freshness_caveat: Coal data stale (133 days old, as of 2025-12-26); Clean Dark stale (128 days old, as of 2025-12-31). GB Power, DE Power, Clean Spark, EUA, TTF, Renewables, and Storage are current (0–2 days old as of 2026-05-07–08). Coal price action and clean dark spread reliability compromised; merit-order conclusions rest on current Clean Spark and power reads.
risk_flags: GB Power 93rd percentile (126.88 EUR/MWh); renewable share collapsed to 18th pctile (27% of load) — merit order fully exposed to gas/coal.
Storage 12.4 pp below 5-yr seasonal — refill pace determines summer/autumn power curve trajectory.
Coal stale (133 days old, 8th pctile at $96/t); Clean Dark at median but offset by historically high Clean Spark (92nd pctile, +23.73 EUR/MWh month-on-month) — gas in-the-money vs coal regime intact.
Iran–Hormuz disruption reducing crude shock; methane rule exemptions lower gas-import cost friction — both bearish-power, but Libya ramp timeline and exemption scope remain binary unknowns.

base.summary: Renewables stabilize mid-20s % of load; storage inches higher; TTF midpoint holds. GB premium tightens modestly as supply diversification talk eases geopolitical premium.

downside.summary: Hormuz fully reopens; Libyan gas ramps ahead of schedule; EU methane exemptions broad and swift. TTF eases; renewables recover; storage refill accelerates. Power curve softens.

upside.summary: Libya gas ramp delayed or Iran escalation re-disrupts Suez; renewables stay low; storage refill stalls. TTF and GB power spike on supply deficit and merit-order extension.

themes: Extreme GB power premium; renewables collapse driving merit order
Geopolitical supply diversification—Libya gas, Hormuz transit reopening
EU policy loosening on methane; supply-cost friction abating
Storage deficit (34.3% vs seasonal) keeps H2 refill risk elevated
top_drivers: Renewable share collapse to 18th pctile (−32.8% day-on-day) forces 100% gas/coal dispatch; GB Power 93rd pctile reflects tight Irish Sea wind + storage deficit.

Geopolitical supply diversification (Libya pivot, Hormuz reopening, methane exemptions) eases macro TTF pressure but adds execution risk; Libya ramp feasibility and exemption scope are key binary unknowns.

Clean Spark at 92nd pctile (+23.73 EUR/MWh month, +78.9 weekly) shows sustained gas-in-the-money regime despite EUA flat (26th pctile, −1.47% yesterday) — coal too cheap to compete, but storage refill (11th pctile) limits sustained high power.
top_takeaway: GB Power at 93rd percentile amid tight renewables (18th pctile) and low storage (11th pctile); Hormuz reopening eases crude-TTF arb but geopolitical tail-risk remains; watch Libya gas ramp and EU methane exemption scope.

watchlist.why: GB at 93rd percentile (126.88) vs DE at 76th (138); tight Irish Sea wind, storage stress, and policy divergence drive sustained GB–DE spread. Monitor daily flow risk.
Collapsed -32.8% day-on-day to 18th pctile (27% of load); gas must fill margin. Track 10-day wind/solar forecast for merit-order repricing.
At 34.3% (11th pctile), 12.4 pp below seasonal; Q2–Q3 injection rate will set summer power floor and autumn bulk-generation fuel switching.
Italy–Libya pipeline ramp-up is key supply backstop under Suez/Hormuz stress; production feasibility and commissioning date critical to TTF supply-demand rebalancing.
Pending Commission guidance on oil/gas firm exemptions; broader exemption → higher gas flows, lower TTF marginal cost, weaker EU power curve.
US military stabilization reducing supply-shock premium; lower Brent weaker coal-to-gas switch incentive; monitor next 2 weeks for trade repricing.

watchlist_dated.name: EU Commission methane exemption guidance expected
watchlist_dated.why: Release scope and timeline determines oil/gas cost relief; direct TTF and power merit-order impact.
